perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date and automatically renewing annually thereafter, unless either party provides a thirty-day notice of written termination one to the other ("Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date and automatically renewing annually thereafter, unless either party provides a sixty-day notice of written termination one to the other ("Term").
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the termination notice period from 30 days to 60 days. This directly contradicts the clause in Section 10, which defines the conditions for termination, especially where a 10-day notice is required for termination for cause. This change introduces uncertainty regarding the actual required notice period, creating potential disputes over termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. ESSI shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Talent in the event Talent fails to perform the duties set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Such termination shall relieve ESSI of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination notice period from 30 days to 60 days, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which specifies a 10-day notice period for termination by ESSI for cause. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the required notice period for termination, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Payments shall be made to Talent in the following manner, and to the following address:
perturbation.changed_text: Payments shall be made to Talent within 90 days of invoice submission, and to the following address:
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a payment deadline of 90 days from invoice. There is no payment timeline currently mentioned in the text, so the introduction of '90 days of invoice submission' adds a contradiction, because the text does not explain how payment is handled currently. This causes conflict with termination clause that dictates a 10-day timeline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. Talent shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to ESSI in the event of the occurrence of any of the following: (1) ESSI adjudicates as insolvent or declares bankruptcy; or (2) ESSI fails to provide consideration due pursuant to this Agreement, within ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due hereunder, provided that ESSI is notified in writing of such non-payment by Talent and such payment by ESSI is not made within three (3) days following such notification; or (3) ESSI breaches any representation, warranty, covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Furthermore, ESSI agrees that such termination shall not relieve it of its obligation to provide consideration as contemplated hereunder. Talent shall not have waived any of its rights at law or in equity by exercising any provision of this paragraph.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a payment deadline of 90 days from invoice submission, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that allows Talent to terminate the agreement if ESSI fails to provide consideration within ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due. This creates a direct conflict in payment timelines, as one section dictates 90 days for payment while another allows termination after only 10 days of non-payment, thus causing an inconsistency as defined above.
